content:
# Alat Aquifer

**Summary**: A secondary aquifer of porous, karstified and fissured limestone.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] modeled the Alat aquifer to determine its hydraulic properties and thickness.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] characterized the alat-aquifer to determine its hydrogeochemical properties.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] marine

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] characterized the alat-aquifer to determine its hydrogeochemical properties.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] karstification

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The thickness varies from 36 to 57 meters in the study area. ([[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995, p. 55]])

- The Alat aquifer has a transmissivity ranging from 2.6 x 10^2 to 2.3 x 10^3 m^2/s. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 44]])
